question: How many vacation days do full-time employees get per year?
answer: I don't have enough information in our HR policies to answer that question.
contexts:

time will accrue.
Regular part-time employees will be eligible for (insert amount here) of paid vacation per employment year.
If your employment is terminated, either voluntarily or involuntarily, you will be paid for unused vacation leave.

hours on a monthly basis, and will be required to take five consecutive business days of vacation each calendar year in

Beginning on the first of the month following 30 days of continuous employment with the company, all full-time
employees are eligible to accrue sick time. This sick time accrues at the rate of one half-day per calendar month through
Dec. 31. Beginning Jan. 1 of the first full year of full-time employment, you are allowed sick time to a maximum of six
days per calendar year. These days are accrued pro-rata based on the actual percentage of the calendar year you work.
ground_truth: The Vacation Policy requires five consecutive business days of vacation each calendar year (after the third month of employment) and states that regular full-time employees receive a set amount of paid vacation per employment year, but the handbook leaves that amount as a template placeholder rather than a fixed number. PTO is handled separately: employees accrue PTO after 3 months of employment and employee PTO is capped at 40 hours.

question: How many sick leave days are allowed per year?
answer: According to Section: Time Away From Work in our Sick Time Policy, a full-time employee is allowed sick time to a maximum of six days per calendar year, starting from the first full year of full-time employment.
contexts:
Beginning on the first of the month following 30 days of continuous employment with the company, all full-time
employees are eligible to accrue sick time. This sick time accrues at the rate of one half-day per calendar month through
Dec. 31. Beginning Jan. 1 of the first full year of full-time employment, you are allowed sick time to a maximum of six
days per calendar year. These days are accrued pro-rata based on the actual percentage of the calendar year you work.

who are too injured or ill to work, not as additional personal or vacation time. Employees should be prepared to furnish a
doctor’s note or similar evidence of inability to work if the supervisor requests one. Abuse of sick leave is grounds for
discipline, up to and including termination.
Extended Leave
Employees who require three or more days off due their own health issue or a health issue of a family member may be

Sick time may not be carried over from year to year. If you are away from work for less than half of a day, the option of a
half sick day may be used, or the missed amount of time may be made up during that workweek if the work schedule
permits and your supervisor approves.
Sick time is a benefit provided to you in the event you need to take time off because of your health or the health of a
ground_truth: Beginning Jan. 1 of the first full year of full-time employment, full-time employees may use sick time up to a maximum of six days per calendar year (accrued pro-rata). Before that, sick time accrues at one half-day per calendar month through Dec. 31 starting the first of the month following 30 days of continuous employment.

ground_truth: The first 90 days of employment are an orientation period: the employee receives training and orientation as directed, the supervisor monitors performance, and the employee may be terminated during this time if performance is unsatisfactory. A confidential performance evaluation is given at the end of the orientation period.

question: At what rate is overtime paid?
answer: According to our Overtime Pay Policy, Section: General Practices (Source 1), all hourly, non-exempt employees must be compensated one-and-a-half times their regular wage rate for all hours worked in excess of 40 hours each week.
contexts:

The company’s workweek begins on 5:00 PM on Friday and ends on 9:00 AM on Monday.
At times, employees will be asked to work overtime to complete necessary work tasks. The employee’s supervisor will
notify the employee as early as possible regarding scheduling needs.

employment.

Employees who want to work more than 40 hours during a workweek must receive written authorization from their
supervisor before working overtime.
(EMPLOYER NOTE: State wage and hour laws differ—check with your legal advisor.)
ground_truth: Hourly, non-exempt employees must be compensated one-and-a-half times their regular wage rate for all hours worked in excess of 40 hours each week.

question: What is VanaciPrime's policy on at-will employment?
answer:
According to Source 1 | Policy: Welcome and Purpose | Section: Introduction, employment with VanaciPrime is on an “at-will” basis, which means that either an employee or VanaciPrime may terminate the employment relationship at any time, for any reason, with or without cause.

Additionally, Source 3 | Policy: Welcome and Purpose | Section: Introduction states that only a written agreement, signed by the President of VanaciPrime can change the at-will nature of the employment of any individual.
contexts:
employment decisions including hiring, promotion, development opportunities, pay or benefits. We offer fair treatment
of employees based on merit and comply with all applicable federal, state and local labor laws.
Employment with VanaciPrime is on an “at-will” basis, which means that either an employee or VanaciPrime may terminate the employment
relationship at any time, for any reason, with or without cause. This handbook is not a contract of employment nor is it

and to help answer many of the questions that may arise in connection with employment at .
I understand that this handbook and any other provisions contained in it do not constitute a guarantee of employment
or an employment contract, express or implied. I understand that VanaciPrime is an “at will” employer and as such, employment with
is not for a fixed term or definite period and may be terminated at the will of either party, with or without cause, and

intended to create contractual obligations for the company of any kind or alter the at-will employment relationships
between VanaciPrime and our employees. Only a written agreement, signed by the President of VanaciPrime can change the at-will nature of the
employment of any individual.
The policies and procedures outlined will be applied at the discretion of VanaciPrime management. As such, may deviate from the
ground_truth: VanaciPrime is an at-will employer: employment is not for a fixed term and may be terminated at the will of either party, with or without cause and without prior notice, and the handbook does not constitute an employment contract.

question: What are the standard working hours at VanaciPrime?
answer: According to our Online Social Networking Policy | Section: General Practices, the standard working hours at VanaciPrime are from 9:00 AM to 9:00 AM, Monday through Friday.
contexts:
The standard workweek at VanaciPrime consists of 40 hours and starts on Monday at 9:00 AM and ends on Friday at 5:00 PM. Employees will be paid on a semi-monthly basis. Regular paydays occur on the 15th and last day of each month. If paydays fall on non-workdays or holidays, employees will be paid on the last workday prior to the
regularly scheduled payday.
If an employee is absent on the date of paycheck distribution, his or her check will be held until he or she returns.

Unless you are informed otherwise, always assume that VanaciPrime is open for business during normal hours. Use common sense
and your best judgment, however, when traveling to work in severe weather.
Some types of severe weather include blizzards, hurricanes and tornadoes.
If the company is not going to open for the day, either you will be emailed, or the information will appear on the
company’s website. If this happens, you will be compensated for your entire work day.

given by a manager or supervisor. This can be done either through a specific company account on a given social
networking site or through a personal account set up for the purposes of recruiting or marketing for .
The term Working Hours includes any time employees are being paid to conduct company business. Standard working
hours are from 9:00 AM to 9:00 AM, Monday through Friday. This timeframe may vary based on job type and
responsibilities.
Procedures
Prohibited Use
ground_truth: The standard workweek is 40 hours, starting Monday at 9:00 AM and ending Friday at 5:00 PM. (Some other sections repeat template errors such as “9:00 AM to 9:00 AM.”)

The standard workweek at VanaciPrime consists of 40 hours and starts on Monday at 9:00 AM and ends on Friday at 5:00 PM. Employees will be paid on a semi-monthly basis. Regular paydays occur on the 15th and last day of each month. If paydays fall on non-workdays or holidays, employees will be paid on the last workday prior to the
regularly scheduled payday.
If an employee is absent on the date of paycheck distribution, his or her check will be held until he or she returns.
ground_truth: Employees are paid on a semi-monthly basis; regular paydays are on the 15th and last day of each month.

question: Does VanaciPrime conduct drug testing?
answer:
Yes, VanaciPrime conducts drug testing. 

[Source 4 | Policy: Drug and Alcohol Testing Policy | Section: Workplace Conduct]
Any individual who conducts business for VanaciPrime or applies for a position with the company is covered by our drug-free workplace policy that includes drug testing.

[Source 5 | Policy: Drug and Alcohol Testing Policy | Section: Workplace Conduct]
Laboratory Testing
All drug and/or alcohol testing will be conducted in a laboratory certified by Department of Health and Human Services (HHS), according to the following procedures...
contexts:
reason for why the test came back positive, then the positive test result will be reported to VanaciPrime as negative.
Use of Prescription Medications
Nothing in this policy prohibits the appropriate use of prescription medication as legally prescribed by a licensed
physician. If an employee is taking prescription medication with potential side effects that may infringe on the safety of

this form, a retest will be requested. An employee who refuses to sign after it is requested of him or her will be
considered as having refused testing and will be subject to disciplinary action.
Laboratory Testing
All drug and/or alcohol testing will be conducted in a laboratory certified by Department of Health and Human Services
(HHS), according to the following procedures: (1) specimens will be screened for amphetamines, benzoylecgonine

(cocaine), opiates, phencyclidine (PCP) and tetrahydrocannabinol (THC or marijuana); and (2) test results will be
confirmed by gas chromatography/mass spectrometry (GC/MS). reserves the right to test for other substances as well.
ground_truth: Yes. Testing includes pre-employment testing, periodic group testing, annual random testing, reasonable suspicion testing, post-accident testing, and follow-up testing. Employees are subject to drug and alcohol testing at any time, with or without notice, under the Drug and Alcohol Testing Policy.

ground_truth: The handbook does not define a separate annual bank of “personal days.” Paid Time Off (PTO) can be used for personal business and similar needs; PTO accrues after 3 months of employment and is capped at 40 hours. Funeral leave for an immediate family member’s death is up to three paid days and does not count against PTO, vacation, or sick leave.
